title: Metrics, Validation, and Model Selection
summary: Choosing the right metric and validation strategy is as important as the model itself.
key_concepts: Classification: accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC, PR-AUC
Regression: MAE, MSE, RMSE, R², MAPE
Imbalanced classes: use F1, PR-AUC, not accuracy
Confusion matrix: TP, FP, TN, FN
ROC curve: TPR vs FPR at different thresholds
ml_connection: Choosing the wrong metric is a top cause of ML project failure in industry. ROC-AUC is the standard for ranking models.
exercises: Implement precision, recall, F1 from scratch.
Implement ROC curve and AUC from scratch without sklearn.
When would you use PR-AUC over ROC-AUC?
A model has 99% accuracy on a dataset with 1% positive class. Is it good?
code_example:
import numpy as np

def precision_recall_f1(y_true, y_pred):
    tp = np.sum((y_pred == 1) & (y_true == 1))
    fp = np.sum((y_pred == 1) & (y_true == 0))
    fn = np.sum((y_pred == 0) & (y_true == 1))
    precision = tp / (tp + fp + 1e-9)
    recall = tp / (tp + fn + 1e-9)
    f1 = 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall + 1e-9)
    return precision, recall, f1

def roc_auc(y_true, y_scores):
    thresholds = np.sort(np.unique(y_scores))[::-1]
    tprs, fprs = [], []
    for t in thresholds:
        y_pred = (y_scores >= t).astype(int)
        tp = np.sum((y_pred == 1) & (y_true == 1))
        fp = np.sum((y_pred == 1) & (y_true == 0))
        fn = np.sum((y_pred == 0) & (y_true == 1))
        tn = np.sum((y_pred == 0) & (y_true == 0))
        tprs.append(tp / (tp + fn + 1e-9))
        fprs.append(fp / (fp + tn + 1e-9))
    auc = np.trapz(tprs, fprs)
    return abs(auc)
thinking_questions: Why does ROC-AUC not change with class imbalance but accuracy does?
When is recall more important than precision? Give a real example.
